skill.name: explore-run
skill.description: Plans bounded exploratory experiment runs for deep learning research repositories. It ranks candidate variants, applies budget limits, and records fair-comparison caveats for human review.
skill.summary: Plan bounded exploratory experiment runs with ranked variants and comparison caveats.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 45 potential issues, but manual review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, or credential handling. The weak cryptography, shell backtick, path traversal, and reconnaissance detections are false positives from documentation, markdown code spans, and argparse text. The remaining concern is low operational risk from local scripts that read and write user-selected JSON paths.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptographic algorithm detections
low_findings.description: Static analysis listed five weak cryptography detections across four unique locations. Manual review found ordinary words in descriptions and command-line help text, with no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain prose or argparse descriptions. No cryptographic operation or weak algorithm implementation appears at those lines.

low_findings.title: False positive: markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis listed 34 Ruby or shell backtick detections. The cited lines use markdown code spans for file names, skill names, identifiers, and output paths, not executable shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are inside markdown documentation. There is no Ruby code, shell interpolation, or command runner at the cited lines.

low_findings.title: False positive: relative documentation paths flagged as traversal
low_findings.description: Static analysis listed six path traversal detections on two documentation lines. The parent-directory segments point to shared reference documents and are not used by code to access arbitrary files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are markdown references, not file access logic. No user input is joined with these paths in the cited documentation.

low_findings.title: False positive: system reconnaissance detection
low_findings.description: The static system reconnaissance finding points to an Avoid section heading. The section lists behavior the skill must avoid and contains no host discovery, scanning, or environment inspection instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are policy text that restricts behavior. They do not request system inspection or collect host information.

low_findings.title: Low risk: user-selected local JSON output path
low_findings.description: The planning script accepts a required input JSON path and an optional output JSON path. This is expected for the utility, but a user could overwrite a local file by choosing a sensitive output path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write behavior is explicit and local. Risk depends on user-supplied paths, so this is operationally relevant but not malicious.

content.value_statement: Deep learning teams need quick experiments without confusing exploratory results with trusted baselines. This skill ranks candidate runs, limits compute cost, and records comparison caveats for review.
actual_capabilities: Builds exploratory variant matrices from JSON specifications.
Ranks candidate runs using cost, success rate, and expected gain.
Prunes candidate lists with max_variants and max_short_cycle_runs limits.
Labels exploratory results as bounded evidence, not trusted proof.
Defines expected outputs under explore_outputs for review artifacts.
Supports handoff to execution-focused skills when runs are authorized.
limitations: It does not perform trusted baseline execution.
It does not replace conservative training verification.
It does not automatically promote exploratory results.
It requires explicit researcher authorization for exploration.

use_cases.title: Short validation sweep
use_cases.description: Compare a small set of learning rates and subset sizes before spending larger compute budgets.

use_cases.title: Idle compute planning
use_cases.description: Prepare ranked short-cycle candidates that can run during available GPU windows.

use_cases.title: Exploration review packet
use_cases.description: Create run summaries and comparability notes before a team decides what to promote.

prompt_templates.title: Create a small sweep
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an exploratory sweep for learning rate and batch size. Keep the run count small and rank candidates by cost efficiency.

prompt_templates.title: Set budget limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate candidate runs with strict limits on total variants and short-cycle runs. Explain which candidates were pruned.

prompt_templates.title: Tune ranking priorities
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan exploratory runs that prioritize expected gain over cost. Include the scoring assumptions and comparison caveats.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare review outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the exploratory run plan, top-run summary, and comparability notes for human review before any baseline promotion.

output_examples.input: A researcher requests a short learning-rate sweep with strict variant limits.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a ranked candidate list, notes pruned options, and marks the results as bounded exploratory evidence.

output_examples.input: A team wants to compare transfer learning heads during idle GPU time.
output_examples.output: The skill prioritizes candidates by expected gain, highlights fairness limits, and prepares review artifacts under the exploration output area.

output_examples.input: A lab lead asks whether one exploratory result should become the baseline.
output_examples.output: The skill explains that promotion needs human review and trusted verification before the baseline changes.

best_practices: Set explicit variant and short-cycle limits before planning runs.
Start with small subsets before heavier exploratory trials.
Review comparability notes before acting on ranked results.
anti_patterns: Do not use it without explicit exploratory authorization.
Do not treat exploratory rankings as verified results.
Do not use it as the full current_research orchestrator.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It plans bounded exploratory runs and ranks candidates for deep learning research workflows.

faq.question: Does it run training jobs directly?
faq.answer: No. It can prepare plans and hand off execution to other authorized skills.

faq.question: Can it verify a trusted baseline?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for exploration, not conservative baseline verification.

faq.question: What outputs should users expect?
faq.answer: Users should expect ranked run summaries, change notes, comparability notes, and status information.

faq.question: When should teams use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a researcher explicitly authorizes small exploratory trials or budget-aware sweeps.

faq.question: Which AI tools support it?
faq.answer: The skill is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
